$schema: Manual overrides for modern job titles that O*NET miscategorizes. Keyed by lowercased title string. The lookup chain checks this file FIRST, before O*NET's Alternate Titles and the LLM fallback. Add entries whenever a demo surfaces a bad O*NET mapping.

product manager.title: Computer and Information Systems Managers
product manager.reasoning: Modern tech product managers oversee digital product development — this matches Computer and Information Systems Managers. O*NET's default mapping to Marketing Managers is outdated.

technical product manager.title: Computer and Information Systems Managers
technical product manager.reasoning: Technical PM role spans engineering coordination and product strategy — fits Computer and Information Systems Managers.

digital product manager.title: Computer and Information Systems Managers
digital product manager.reasoning: Digital product management in tech — NOT marketing product management.

product marketing manager.title: Marketing Managers
product marketing manager.reasoning: Explicitly marketing-focused — Marketing Managers is correct here.

ux designer.title: Web and Digital Interface Designers
ux designer.reasoning: UX designers design digital user experiences and interactions. They work with Figma, wireframes, and user research — a different field from Software Developers or Graphic Designers.

ui designer.title: Web and Digital Interface Designers
ui designer.reasoning: UI design is the visual/interaction layer of digital products — Web and Digital Interface Designers.

product designer.title: Web and Digital Interface Designers
product designer.reasoning: Product designers in tech design digital products end-to-end — a subset of UX/UI.

ux/ui designer.title: Web and Digital Interface Designers
ux/ui designer.reasoning: Combined UX/UI role — Web and Digital Interface Designers.

ux researcher.title: Web and Digital Interface Designers
ux researcher.reasoning: UX research is part of the user-experience design function in most companies; closest O*NET fit.

interaction designer.title: Web and Digital Interface Designers
interaction designer.reasoning: Interaction design is a subset of UX design in most modern tech companies.

growth marketer.title: Marketing Managers
growth marketer.reasoning: Growth marketing is a modern subset of marketing — Marketing Managers is correct.

growth hacker.title: Marketing Managers
growth hacker.reasoning: Growth hacker is marketing-engineering hybrid but primarily marketing. Marketing Managers captures it.

devops engineer.title: Network and Computer Systems Administrators
devops engineer.reasoning: DevOps engineers manage deployment infrastructure — closest fit is Network and Computer Systems Administrators.

site reliability engineer.title: Network and Computer Systems Administrators
site reliability engineer.reasoning: SRE is infrastructure reliability engineering — closest fit is Network and Computer Systems Administrators.

data engineer.title: Database Architects
data engineer.reasoning: Data engineers design and build data pipelines — Database Architects is the closest O*NET match.

ml engineer.title: Data Scientists
ml engineer.reasoning: ML engineers work on machine learning systems. Data Scientists is the closest O*NET classification.

ai engineer.title: Data Scientists
ai engineer.reasoning: AI/ML work classifies under Data Scientists in O*NET.

prompt engineer.title: Software Developers
prompt engineer.reasoning: Prompt engineering is a modern software role — writing software in natural language. Closest O*NET fit is Software Developers.

frontend developer.title: Web Developers
frontend developer.reasoning: Frontend developers build web UIs — Web Developers.

full stack developer.title: Software Developers
full stack developer.reasoning: Full-stack developers build both frontend and backend — Software Developers captures both.

cloud engineer.title: Network and Computer Systems Administrators
cloud engineer.reasoning: Cloud infrastructure work — Network and Computer Systems Administrators.

solutions architect.title: Computer Systems Engineers/Architects
solutions architect.reasoning: Solutions architects design systems — Computer Systems Engineers/Architects.

scrum master.title: Project Management Specialists
scrum master.reasoning: Scrum masters facilitate agile delivery — Project Management Specialists.

engineering manager.title: Computer and Information Systems Managers
engineering manager.reasoning: Engineering managers oversee technical teams — Computer and Information Systems Managers.

cto.title: Computer and Information Systems Managers
cto.reasoning: CTO is the senior technology leader — Computer and Information Systems Managers.

content creator.title: Writers and Authors
content creator.reasoning: Content creators produce written/video content — closest O*NET fit is Writers and Authors.

youtuber.title: Producers and Directors
youtuber.reasoning: YouTubers produce and direct video content — Producers and Directors.

tiktok influencer.title: Public Relations Specialists
tiktok influencer.reasoning: Influencers do audience engagement and brand promotion — Public Relations Specialists.

vtuber.title: Actors
vtuber.reasoning: VTubers perform in character for an audience — Actors.

podcaster.title: Producers and Directors
podcaster.reasoning: Podcasters produce audio content — Producers and Directors.

radio host.title: Broadcast Announcers and Radio Disc Jockeys
